summary: Conjecture 1.2 — the optimal $O(1/\sqrt{n})$ anti-concentration bound for random signed sums landing in an o-minimal set containing no line segment — has been established for the semialgebraic case by Grebennikov and Kwan (2025, arXiv:2505.24699), who prove $\Pr[\xi_1 a_1+\cdots+\xi_n a_n \in S] \le O_S(n^{-1/2})$ via a lattice-point-counting reduction; the paper explicitly states it resolves conjectures of Fox–Kwan–Spink and Kwan–Sauermann. Whether this covers the full conjecture for all o-minimal (not just semialgebraic) structures is not confirmed from the available abstract and HTML.

since_posted.title: Geometric Littlewood–Offord problems via lattice point counting
since_posted.claim: Proves Pr(X in S) = O_S(n^{-1/2}) for semialgebraic sets S containing no line segment (Theorem 1.8), removing the logarithmic factors from prior work and explicitly resolving conjectures of Fox–Kwan–Spink and Kwan–Sauermann.

search_queries: Fox Kwan Spink Littlewood-Offord o-minimal conjecture 2106.04894 follow-up
Littlewood-Offord o-minimal definable sets 1/sqrt(n) bound arxiv 2023 2024 2025
notes: The Grebennikov–Kwan paper (2505.24699, May 2025) claims to resolve Fox–Kwan–Spink conjectures and proves the optimal O(n^{-1/2}) bound for semialgebraic sets; this is a major special case of o-minimal sets. Status is marked 'partial' rather than 'solved' because the fetched content does not confirm the result for all o-minimal structures beyond the semialgebraic case. Confidence is 'medium' due to this ambiguity.
